Title: Author benchmark workflow doc
Description: Document the benchmark loop, HTML acquisition rules, local cache layout, comparison method, gap attribution, and fixture-promotion rules
Acceptance: `docs/benchmark-workflow.md` exists in English and defines a repeatable benchmark process for Firecrawl vs `markmaton`
Test_Method: manual: review docs/benchmark-workflow.md for acquisition rules, cache layout, attribution rules, and promotion rules

Title: Seed initial benchmark matrix
Description: Create the first benchmark matrix with representative page classes, canonical URLs, chosen HTML mode, current Firecrawl behavior, current `markmaton` status, gap attribution, and promotion decision
Acceptance: `docs/benchmark-matrix.md` exists in English and includes the initial benchmark set with explicit comparison fields
Test_Method: manual: review docs/benchmark-matrix.md for page classes, HTML mode, Firecrawl summary, `markmaton` status, gap layer, and fixture decision

Title: Standardize local benchmark cache layout
Description: Create and use a stable local cache convention under `tmp/benchmarks/<slug>/` so repeated benchmark analysis does not require re-fetching live pages
Acceptance: A local cache folder structure is used consistently for benchmark pages and contains HTML plus Firecrawl and `markmaton` output snapshots
Test_Method: manual: create and inspect `tmp/benchmarks/<slug>/` directories with HTML, Firecrawl, `markmaton`, and notes artifacts

Title: Capture article/docs/wiki benchmark artifacts
Description: Capture cached benchmark artifacts for article, docs, and wiki classes using the HTML mode chosen in the matrix
Acceptance: Cached artifacts exist for Yahoo article, MDN docs, and Wikipedia with HTML and output snapshots ready for comparison
Test_Method: manual: cache HTML plus Firecrawl and `markmaton` outputs for each page and verify files exist under `tmp/benchmarks/`

Title: Capture card/jobs/form benchmark artifacts
Description: Capture cached benchmark artifacts for card/list, careers landing, jobs landing, and application form classes
Acceptance: Cached artifacts exist for OpenAI engineering, OpenAI careers, Thermo Fisher jobs, Thermo Fisher apply, and Ashby apply
Test_Method: manual: cache HTML plus Firecrawl and `markmaton` outputs for each page and verify files exist under `tmp/benchmarks/`

Title: Capture thread/repo/timeline/product benchmark artifacts
Description: Capture cached benchmark artifacts for discussion, repo/app shell, timeline, and product/commerce classes
Acceptance: Cached artifacts exist for Hacker News, GitHub repo, GitHub issue, and Apple product pages
Test_Method: manual: cache HTML plus Firecrawl and `markmaton` outputs for each page and verify files exist under `tmp/benchmarks/`

Title: Complete behavioral comparison notes
Description: Update the matrix with concrete benchmark observations for all captured pages using the behavioral rubric
Acceptance: Each benchmark row records current Firecrawl behavior, `markmaton` behavior, primary gap layer, and short comparison notes
Test_Method: manual: update `docs/benchmark-matrix.md` so every benchmark row has completed notes and attribution

Title: Curate regression fixture shortlist
Description: Choose which sampled pages should remain local-only and which page patterns deserve promotion into `testdata/fixtures/regression/`
Acceptance: The matrix contains explicit keep-local vs promote decisions, and only representative parser patterns are shortlisted
Test_Method: manual: review matrix decisions and confirm each promoted candidate corresponds to a reusable parser failure mode
Notes: Do not promote every sampled page

Title: Derive next parser slice by layer
Description: Translate benchmark findings into a layer-based refinement recommendation rather than a site-by-site patch list
Acceptance: A concise next-slice recommendation exists and groups follow-up work under `cleanhtml`, `postprocess`, `convert/core`, and `quality`
Test_Method: manual: review benchmark findings and produce a layer-based refinement summary without site-specific patching

Title: Validate benchmark planning artifacts
Description: Validate that the benchmark plan and issue CSV are well-formed and aligned with the benchmark docs
Acceptance: The plan file exists, the CSV passes validation, and the docs/CSV agree on comparison mode, cache policy, and promotion rules
